mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware and model types. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of configuration for specific use cases.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM from the official source.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM Helm repository.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes. This step ensures that the required CRDs are installed.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the vLLM CRDs.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in a specified namespace using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM custom resource to deploy a model for inference. It is essential to test the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample vLLM resource for a GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM custom resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM resources in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes vLLM from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, and any data stored in PVCs may be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the vLLM namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step involves upgrading the vLLM installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM resource is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the vLLM Helm chart is installed in the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs for vLLM are already installed in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Skip the CRD installation step if they already exist.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the model configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits for the cluster or reduce the resource requests in the vLLM configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved performance for existing models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource in a 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.

kind: VLLM
metadata:

spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
